duration: 75 minutes (45 minutes on the 20th, 30 minutes on the 23rd)
description: The Western Gull was originally located by Floyd and Sibley at Smith Reservoir in Costilla Co. in early June; the Rawinski's relocated the WEGU at the Blanca Wetlands, and once Blanca opened to the public I tried for it. I observed the bird on two seperate occasions, once on July 20th and again on July 23rd. On the 20th the bird was on Pond 115, resting on a sandy island in the middle of the pond with coots and Am. Avocets. I observed the bird through a 15-45x60 scope, as the bird preened and rested. The mantle of the bird was dark slaty gray, noticeably darker than any of the CAGUs on the pond. The legs were pink, the eye pale and orbital ring yellow. The bill was thick and stout with a red gonydeal spot. On the 23rd, the bird had moved to the large body of water to the east of the N. Mallard parking area. The bird was more distant on this observation, but was feeding next to a few California and Ring-billed Gulls, allowing for a nice size comparison. The WEGU was slightly larger than the CAGUs. I photographed the WEGU on both days, although others have posted much better photos than mine.
